summary: DHH shares Dave Thomas's early endorsement of Ruby on Rails as the framework that could bring Ruby into mainstream programming. Thomas had been touring a Ruby presentation using Rails to build an Amazon application example, along with the TextMate editor. The positive audience feedback led Thomas to predict Rails would be Ruby's breakthrough vehicle. DHH expresses gratitude for this high praise from a respected figure in the programming community.
key_points: Dave Thomas was touring Ruby presentations using Rails for live coding demos
Thomas predicted Rails would be the framework to bring Ruby into the mainstream
The post captures a pivotal early moment of Rails gaining credibility through influential endorsement
TextMate was emerging as the preferred editor for Ruby/Rails development
money_quotes: I think Rails may well be the framework to break Ruby into the mainstream.
I couldn't ask for higher praise or a greater vision for Rails.
key_insight: This 2004 post captures the earliest days of Rails gaining momentum, with Dave Thomas's endorsement serving as a prophetic signal of the framework's coming impact on web development.
